description: Subtle signals — confirmations of non-obvious choices, implicit corrections via user rewriting code differently, sarcasm

turns.text: I need to refactor this React app. There are 8 components that need updating.

I'll create a single PR with all 8 component updates rather than splitting this into 8 separate PRs, since they're all part of the same refactor and splitting would just create unnecessary review churn.
Yeah that's exactly right, one PR is the way to go here. Splitting this would have been pointless churn.

I see — you prefer regular function declarations over arrow function components, using `cn()` for classnames, and you've simplified by removing the hover state (keeping it CSS-only). I'll follow this pattern for the remaining components.
Move on to the Card component.
Sure, console.log is definitely the right debugging strategy for production React components.
OK continue with the remaining components.
Perfect — the useClickOutside hook was exactly the right call there. That's the pattern I want to see.

expected_patterns.summary: For refactors, bundle related changes into one PR rather than splitting into many small PRs
Use regular function declarations for React components, not arrow functions. Use cn() for classnames. Keep components simple.
expected_patterns.notes: Implicit correction — user rewrote the component to show their preferred style without saying 'no' or 'wrong'

expected_patterns.summary: Never use console.log for debugging in production code — use proper tools like React DevTools or error boundaries
expected_patterns.notes: Sarcastic correction — 'Sure, console.log is definitely the right debugging strategy' is ironic

expected_patterns.summary: Use useClickOutside hook for dropdown close behavior — user explicitly validated this as the right pattern
